w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: This is the first of 8 videos. When we refer to elements of a trade setup, there are really just two primary concerns and they are listed below
Context or framework surrounding the idea:
This is what makes the idea

for a trade and not simply what your indicators or support and resistance level says. There should be something that builds a reason to want to trade. We will be learning specific principles and we are going to be dealing with them in
general terms and what we do in those conditions. These conditions are listed below:
Each one of these four gives a specific framework and a context to the marketplace that we are going to be trading in. There is going to be either one of the four conditions.
s the algorithm that controls market movement. It works with a set of rules just like any computer program.
There is going to be times where the market goes
same as consolidation). When this happens, the market will be looking to do an expansion. All markets start from a consolidation and move into an
that’s an impulse price swing). After that impulse
either goes to a consolidation again or it goes to a retracement. When a retracement
goes back down into another level of expansion or after

expansion, it can go to a reversal pattern, after the reversal pattern, it will see another retracement then back to potentially consolidation. These four conditions interchange throughout the marketplace. You are only going to get one of these four conditions. You just need to know where the market is at right now, where it is likely to go and where it came from. Throughout the course, you are going to learn a lot about where the market is going next and that’s going to fill in a lot of gaps.

The main thing, the consolidation begins with everything. All the moves that take place in the marketplace start with a measure of consolidation. That’s where the market builds orders. The market maker keeps market in a tight range or defined range until there is enough money on both parts of the upper and lower ends of the range is being defined by the consolidation. Whichever one has the highest amount of money to be absorbed, that’s the direction it is going to move in.

We don’t always know what that is but we wait for the expansion, when the expansion occurs, that’s when we get the clue as to what the market is most likely going to be doing and wait for a retracement or another consolidation or
always wait for the first expansion which gives us all the insights that we need to make a decision. Sometimes it may expand so far that we can’t do anything with it that we have to wait for the
retracement or the next consolidation. There is nothing wrong with that, it is all

are not going to catch every move. The main thing is understanding these four individual characteristics to a trade setup because price is delivered by one of these four conditions. It can’t be any other way.
Expansion is when price moves quickly from a level of equilibrium. Expansion couples directly with the to
The Importance of Expansion; When price leaves a
level quickly, this indicates a willingness on the part of the market makers to reveal their intended repricing model. Now what does that mean?
we are in a consolidation or point of equilibrium, if price wants to move up quickly, that will give us an indication of looking for a bullish
, we don’t want to chase price, we want to wait for price to come back down into the
. Where is that going to occur? Well, what do we look for in price? The order block that the market makers leave near or at equilibrium price point.
As you can see here, there’s a consolidation in the blue shaded area. This is a very
defined consolidation. It’s got a clear
high and low and the equilibrium price point is directly in the middle of the high and the low end of that range. You can simply take the Fibonacci
lay the fib on the high and the low in the general consolidation to find that midpoint. The market will eventually move outside of the consolidation
. You can see that impulse move in the yellow-like shaded box. It moves away from the equilibrium price point and all we have to do is go back
to the down candle right before that up move. That down
black candle) is the bullish order block. When the price comes back down into that and hits it that’s where we would be buying.
you can see it hits that level and expands to the up side.
Retracement is when price moves back inside the recently created price range.

The Importance of Retracement: When price returns inside a recent price range, this indicates a willingness on the part of the market makers to reprice to levels not efficiently traded for fair value. When we are thinking retracement, the go to is for ICT tools we are looking for liquidity gaps and liquidity voids. When we look for price when we see run ups

quick and run downs in price in other words real quick rallies up or real quick rallies down in price many times that range that’s created will want to come back in and close that in. Find an example below:
This is an example of a retracement
. As you can see here the orange shaded
we have a real quick sudden movement away from a price level and that quick sudden movement creates what we call as a liquidity void. What we’ll do is we’ll wait as a trader, we wont chase price, we
and say okay there’s going to be either an indication that get long and try to fill in that range or we can wa
for the come all the way back to it and fill in the liquidity void. Once it hits it then it will probably resume going lower and that’s what we’re looking for in terms of liquidity void.
Reversal is when price moves the opposite direction the current direction has taken in.
if we are looking for reversals, we are directly coupling that with an ICT tool of liquidity pools.
The Importance of Reversal: When the price reverses direction, it indicates the market makers have
level of stops and a significant move should unfold in the new direction.
What to look for in price: The liquidity pools just above an old price high and just below an old price low
The screenshot above shows examples of
‘x’ indicates where stops would be and

the market goes just above those levels and rejects and goes the other way or goes just below those levels where there’s and ‘x’ and rejects and goes the other way. Look how many times there’s so many opportunities just on this one chart.
Consolidation is when price moves inside a clear trading range and shows no willingness to move significantly higher or lower.
The Importance of Consolidation: When price consolidates, it indicates the market makers are allowing orders to build on both sides of the market. Expect a new Expansion near term.
What we look for in price: We are waiting for the impulse move or impu
se swing in price away from the equilibrium price level that is found exactly in the halfway point of the consolidation range.
We see here where we’ve identified a range defined specifically by the bodies of the candles not the wicks as you can see, price moves out
in an expansive manner and then comes back
to the equilibrium price point and then to the

having an understanding of these specific characteristics and elements of trading a setup you’ll give yourself framework to first learn how to practice and study price action and eventually work towards understanding
How Market Makers Condition the Market
This is the second teaching for month 1, the image above is the market efficiency paradigm and what this generally is depicting

of the larger circle of uninformed money and whether we acknowledge it or not, there is a smart money group of traders out there. When we as new traders look as the market as new traders, we may or may not have the understanding that there is a smart money entity out there and we as the larger populace of retail minded trading, we think because of our sheer vastness we are the driver of this apparatus that’s called the marketplace and we tend to think of ourselves as the drive axle of what makes the markets go up and down and that’s the facade of supply and demand, that’s the facade of trendlines driving price when it touches it, that’s the facade of moving averages, crossovers causing price go up and
the facade that’s perpetuated and we are led to believe that that’s exactly what takes place either in books or in seminars or in webinars or from gurus.
The smart money consists of banks and the uninformed money consists of everybody in retail account.

that the sheer vastness and the size of them is much more controlling in terms of where price is going to be driven higher or lower because of the buying and selling pressure that’s equated to their mere involvement in price and that’s a facade.

Fact is, the small little group of traders which form the smart money are the ones that influence the entire mechanism that we call the markets. This small group of traders is actually the drive shaft. It is the actual motor spinning the whole price higher or lower and not the big circle of uninformed money. Market is not efficient for uninformed money but it is efficient for smart money. The banks drive price whether you want to accept it or not, that’s what’s going on and the sooner you get to that understanding and expectation of what it is that’s going on in price, it’s not for your well-being, it’s for the bank’s well-being.
It’s their business, they are the liquidity provider. Everyone else is liquidity. It is better to join smart money and by the end of course, we will be part of smart money.
In the first video, we learnt elements of a trade setup and the two characteristics to it:
Context and framework around the trade
Reference points in institutional order flow
We have to be able to bridge the two, understanding expansion, retracement, reversal and consolidation and applying them to respectively order blocks, fair value
What does that mean for us for instance in the scope of price? How can we use these ideas and make them applicable to price?
expansion, retracement, reversal and consolidation mean something. Everyday starts with

Asian Range) after midnight there’s a manipulation that takes place and that’s expansion and it comes in a form of a judas swing. What’s it doing? It’s making the higher low in London and that’s the London swing for a reversal , that’s a run on stops, there is another expansion move down into the New York Session and there’s going to be another consolidation, that’s the new York consolidation going into the 8:00-8:30 news embargo lift where there is going to be another injection of liquidity or a reversal, then there will be another expansion and we go into London close which is another reversal condition and then what
market goes in consolidation for the rest of the day.
we have a way of looking at these things and applying these concepts to time of the day and repeating characteristics.

The daily range structure can be really broken down and simplified. It starts with a price equilibrium, that’s the Asian range, then there’s a manipulation and that’s always going to come by some news events either at the time of the manipulation or just before it, that’s the judas swing. Then we’ll see a range expansion, in order words after the higher low is formed, the range will start expanding. It will go down into 5:00AM New York time or go up into that time window depending upon the daily direction. We’re going to use the perspective it’s a buy
means the Asian range had a small consolidation and then right after midnight New York time there’s a drop down in price, that’s the

for the low). There’s a range expansion, then it goes into the reversal, that’s classic London open scenario where it shoots down, runs the stops and then expands again. There is another range expansion 5:00 in the morning
time. Between 5:00 in the morning to 8:00 in the morning in that time
market will go back into consolidation, then it’ll have a retracement 8:00AM and 8:30AM
time. Then it’ll have either a reversal in
. The range will expand and make rest of the day going up into 10:00 or 11:00 in the morning
time where it’ll have a reversal again, that’s the London close, then the market will go into consolidation ending true day at 19001 Forex LTDs platform.

we can see the interbank price delivery algorithm on a daily basis by studying these events and seeing over a period of time you’ll know what is most likely to happen. Now let’s look at it a little bit further.
If we are looking at price delivery and this is the model that comes by way of my ICT’s

having interbank feeds price to everyone. If we’re focusing on delivery of price, we have to understand that it all starts with a consolidation. Nothing can happen until consolidation. Consolidation is when the market’s quiet. It is important because that’s when orders are building up in the marketplace. The market makers will allow orders to build up above and below the
The next stage is always expansion. It’s not consolidation to
it can’t retrace if it hasn’t moved anywhere. It can’t go from consolidation to reversal because it has to come out of the consolidation.
when you see a consolidation or holding

got to think the next leg in price is going to be an explosive move or an impulse leg or an Impulse price swing. You need to see movement. By determining what that movement is in what direction is relative to the conditions you’re trading in. Once we are in the expansion stage, then you have a choice. It can retrace and come back to the
it just left behind and recapitalize that and then make another leg up or down relative to the
direction it’s moved, or once it’s moved into expansion, it can reverse. Once it reverses, there will be another expansion, then it goes back to a consolidation. The main thing
want you to understand is it never goes

it never does consolidation to reversal. It’s always consolidation to expansion and then from expansion it goes to either retracement or reversal. It does not do consolidation to expansion and consolidation. It does not happen. It does a consolidation to
either retraces for another move into an order block and then recapitalize it and do the same direction it moved when it made an expansion or it goes from expansion to reversal.
When you understand this algorithm, the way it moves and the way it
very systematic. It has only a few options to go to and they’re time sensitive. They are day sensitive. They are intermarket related so when one market is moving
fast, if you understand the relationships that
am going to teach you over a couple
will know where the trade’s setting up because one’s being held and the one’s letting it run which is the importance of knowing what the euro
All these things are going to blend together and you’ll know exactly how these things are done.

There is a process that we are going to learn that is very generic, it will not break, it won’t stop working and when you look at the marketplace in your charts, it doesn’t matter what timeframe you are looking at, the daily range was just used to teach how easy it is to see a repeating format. So simple, everyday you can

opportunity to study and see how it works. That same thing happens with a weekly range. The weekly range is the same thing, Sunday is open consolidation, then there’s an expansion move in Monday, then there’s a reversal on Tuesday or Monday and there’s another expansion move then it goes back to consolidation midweek and it’s going to reverse or retrace.
What You Should Be focusing on right now:
Create Daily Price Action logs with price charts: ICT said this is a non negotiable and this is strength in the development process.
Daily chart-You should start with the daily chart with 12 months worth of chart
the bare minimum should be 9 months.
Don’t try forecasting price movement right now
Do-Note where price shown a quick movement from a specific level
Do-Note recent highs and recent lows that haven’t been retested or traded back to
Do-Note areas on charts where price has left ‘clean’ highs or
that looks like two equal highs that formed in close proximity to one another. When we see a high go up and form and then it trades away from it for a little while

and comes right back to it and it doesn’t make a new high or maybe it falls just a little bit shorter or just a little bit above it. I note that as a clean high and usually buy stops will form above that and the market will usually come back up there and run through that. It doesn’t mean it won’t continue through it but it’s usually a big bullseye for price to want to go up into that
area and the reverse is said for double

or equal lows. When a low is formed and another low is equally formed in close proximity to the initial one, that’s a big area for sell stops to pull or build up underneath those lows and the market tends to have the willingness to go down and test that liquidity. That means the market will
go down into that area whether it continues to go lower or if it goes down
conditions that we look for to frame all that and you will know when to expect the specific conditions but for now,
want you to start practicing looking for that in your charts and having them noted on your charts.
lows form and you want to know what time of day it occurs
it forms in. Is the high and low of the week forming in London or is it forming in New York because all those are going to lend well to prognostication and what should happen going forward
Note the daily high and the daily low every trading day and what
The above screenshot is the daily chart for the USDCHF, ICT recommends we use one currency pair throughout this mentorship.
what you want to first do is to mark
most recent highs and lows where market has shown a willingness to repel from. This is the first thing you want to note

this is how you identify order blocks, liquidity voids, and all the beginning frameworks of that but you need to be able to note those recent highs and recent lows. That’s what has been marked for you in the most recent screenshot above.
Next, you are going to drop to the 4H chart as shown in screenshot below:
Those same levels marked on the daily chart are the ones still showing here. More highs and more lows come into visibility by doing a lower timeframe perspective.
once you move from one chart to the
marked levels are transposed immediately to the other timeframe and that’s what we see here.
Go through the 4H chart to loo

king for areas where it’s 2 clean equal highs and equal lows and in close proximity to one another, these are marked in the 4H chart above. You also look at where the market has moved quickly away from a particular level. When it creates these real big candles or bars on your chart, you want to note that because they are going to be influential in your expectations of where price should go and where they should not go.
You then move down to the hourly chart as shown below:

In the hourly chart, you will be looking at individual days over a course of 1 or 2 weeks and you can get the weekly range defined with the hourly chart and you can look at the intraday highs and lows with an hour chart. The hourly chart is a good bellwether chart if you are a
trader or a day trader that’s like the daily chart for the barometer whether you should be a buyer or a seller.
All these will be taught in details in this mentorship but for now, you will be taking all those levels you found on the daily chart and 4H and transposing those to an hourly chart.
